case_studies.title: Brake Module CNC Consolidation Cuts Line Changeovers
case_studies.summary: Tier-1 automotive supplier consolidated three brake module variants into a common CNC platform, improving OEE and achieving an 18% cost reduction on low-volume SKUs.
case_studies.content: A Tier-1 automotive supplier struggled with low utilization across three separate brake module machining lines. By redesigning fixtures and consolidating the product family into a single flexible CNC cell, they reduced changeover time from 90 minutes to 25 minutes. The project delivered an 18% piece-price cost reduction on low-volume SKUs, a 12% improvement in on-time delivery, and freed up 240 m² of floor space.

case_studies.title: Automotive Cross-Member Line Consolidation Delivers 21% Cost Reduction
case_studies.summary: European OEM consolidated three stamped cross-member designs into a single modular platform and achieved a 21% cost reduction while maintaining crash performance.
case_studies.content: An automotive OEM needed to rationalize regional variants of a front subframe cross-member. Our engineering team standardized hole patterns, introduced modular brackets, and transferred manufacturing to a flexible robotic welding and machining cell. Finite element validation confirmed equivalent crash performance. The program achieved a verified 21% cost reduction, 15% weight reduction, and reduced part numbers from 9 to 3 across global platforms.

case_studies.title: Localized EV Powertrain Housing Machining Saves 24% Total Landed Cost
case_studies.summary: North American EV start-up localized machining of aluminum powertrain housings and realized a 24% total landed cost reduction versus offshore sourcing.
case_studies.content: A fast-growing EV start-up imported machined powertrain housings from overseas, facing long lead times and volatile freight costs. By localizing aluminum 6061 housing machining to our automated cell network, they cut average lead time from 10 weeks to 3 weeks. Optimized toolpaths, high-pressure coolant, and in-line probing reduced scrap by 60%. The project delivered a 24% total landed cost reduction and enabled weekly design revisions during ramp-up.

case_studies.title: High-Pressure Die Casting for EV Motor Brackets Cuts Weight by 19%
case_studies.summary: Conversion from machined steel to high-pressure die cast aluminum motor brackets reduced component weight by 19% and improved assembly ergonomics.
case_studies.content: An EV systems supplier wanted to reduce the mass of an electric motor mounting bracket. We converted the design from machined steel plate to high-pressure die cast aluminum with localized reinforcement. Process simulation eliminated porosity in critical clamp areas. While the primary KPI was weight (19% reduction), the new process also reduced machining time by 35% and improved assembly ergonomics on the final vehicle line.

case_studies.title: Multi-Material Body Structure Strategy Achieves 27% Cost Reduction
case_studies.summary: Global automaker introduced a multi-material body structure strategy and achieved a 27% cost reduction on low-volume performance models.
case_studies.content: A global automaker needed a scalable body structure concept for low-volume performance derivatives. Our team combined ultra-high-strength steel stampings with extruded aluminum crash members and composite reinforcements. Shared joining cells handled mixed-material joining with minimal tooling changes. The program achieved a 27% cost reduction compared to the previous steel-intensive design, while improving torsional stiffness by 11% and meeting all crash requirements on the first physical prototypes.

case_studies.title: Aerospace Titanium Bracket Cell Improves OEE by 32%
case_studies.summary: Tier-1 aerospace machinist deployed a dedicated titanium milling cell, improving OEE by 32% and stabilizing long-run production.
case_studies.content: A Tier-1 aerospace supplier struggled with frequent schedule changes and low spindle utilization on titanium structural brackets. We designed a dedicated titanium milling cell with standardized fixturing, dynamic toolpath optimization, and in-machine probing. Overall equipment effectiveness improved by 32%, scrap was reduced by 40%, and first-pass yield increased to 98.7%.

case_studies.title: Modular Composite Tooling for Wing Components Reduces Changeover by 45%
case_studies.summary: Modular composite vacuum fixtures reduced wing component changeover times by 45% and improved positional repeatability.
case_studies.content: An aerospace OEM producing wing skins and stiffeners used legacy metallic fixtures that required long changeovers. We introduced modular composite vacuum fixtures with quick-locating elements. Changeover time was reduced by 45%, and positional repeatability improved to within ±0.05 mm, supporting a move to lights-out weekend shifts.

case_studies.title: Standardized Aluminum Enclosures Reduce Electronics SKUs by 60%
case_studies.summary: Industrial controls manufacturer standardized aluminum CNC enclosures, cutting unique SKUs by 60% and reducing inventory by 28%.
case_studies.content: An industrial controls manufacturer supported 140+ enclosure variants, driving complexity and inventory. By standardizing to a modular family of aluminum 6061 CNC housings with configurable end caps, we cut SKUs by 60% and reduced finished-goods inventory by 28%. Lead times dropped from 6 weeks to 3 weeks with no impact on thermal performance.

case_studies.title: High-Volume Server Brackets Line Increases Throughput by 40%
case_studies.summary: Datacenter OEM launched a high-volume stamped and formed bracket line, increasing hourly throughput by 40% while holding tight flatness specs.
case_studies.content: A datacenter OEM experienced chronic backlogs on server mounting brackets. We implemented a progressive die stamping process with in-die sensing and an automated vision inspection cell. The new line increased hourly throughput by 40%, reduced rework by 70%, and maintained flatness within 0.15 mm across the product family.

case_studies.title: Consumer Electronics OEM Cuts Mechanical BOM Cost by 22%
case_studies.summary: Consumer electronics OEM redesigned its handheld enclosure and achieved a 22% reduction in mechanical BOM cost across 1.2M units annually.
case_studies.content: A consumer electronics brand sought to reduce the cost of a handheld controller without compromising perceived quality. Through DFM workshops, we consolidated fasteners, replaced machined inserts with molded-in features, and standardized surface finishes across the product family. The result was a 22% reduction in mechanical BOM cost on annual volume exceeding 1.2M units, while field failure rates decreased by 11%.

case_studies.title: Lean Cell for Disposable Medical Sets Improves OTIF to 99.3%
case_studies.summary: Medical device manufacturer deployed a lean packaging cell for disposable sets, improving OTIF performance from 93% to 99.3%.
case_studies.content: A medical device producer faced late shipments on pre-sterilized disposable sets. Working within ISO 13485 constraints, we designed a lean, U-shaped packaging cell with error-proofed pick paths and visual kanban signals. On-time-in-full (OTIF) performance increased from 93% to 99.3%, while operator walking distance per kit was reduced by 37%.

case_studies.title: Pump Manufacturer Shortens Lead Times by 50% with Modular Assembly
case_studies.summary: Industrial pump manufacturer implemented modular assembly and cut standard lead times from 8 weeks to 4 weeks.
case_studies.content: An industrial pump OEM needed to respond faster to custom configuration requests. By redesigning product architecture around shared modules and rebalancing assembly workstations, standard lead times were cut from 8 weeks to 4 weeks. Order-to-build accuracy rose to 99.5%, and field inventory at distributors was reduced by 18%.
